name: Monday Rover (Raspberry Pi)
summary: Monday Rover is a Raspberry Pi robotics project focused on fast iteration across perception and control loops. Marwan built computer-vision-assisted behaviors in prototype form, handled hardware bring-up and sensor/actuator integration, and debugged on-device with tight feedback cycles and observability.
why_it_matters: Demonstrates Marwan's practical approach to delivering reliable, maintainable systems.

name: Generative Video Platform
summary: Generative Video Platform (GVP) explores how generative AI fits real video workflows where latency, repeatability, and UX all matter. Marwan prototypes product UX and pipeline behavior together: inputs, generation, review, and publish with clear boundaries as the system evolves.
why_it_matters: Demonstrates how Marwan combines product UX with clear service boundaries for AI-enabled workflows.

name: Apptio (IBM)
summary: Apptio (IBM) is enterprise TBM and IT financial management SaaS where correctness is non-negotiable. Marwan led a migration to solve scaling and cost: pipelines were running for days on Kubernetes and Java; he moved heavy workloads to EMR Spark with Scala and built a parallel reconciliation pipeline so legacy and new systems ran on the same inputs and flagged discrepancies in real time — roughly 10x faster processing with zero correctness drift and incremental cutover.
why_it_matters: Shows Marwan's approach to platform modernization in data-intensive SaaS with strong operability guardrails.

name: JumpCloud
summary: JumpCloud is a cloud directory platform. Marwan directed end-to-end technical design of a Microsoft M365 integration exposed through GraphQL for 50,000+ tenants, with a hybrid PostgreSQL plus MongoDB layer so relational identity-graph queries stayed fast while per-tenant custom attributes and hierarchies absorbed high write volume.
why_it_matters: Reflects Marwan's work in identity-critical systems where reliability and safe rollout matter.

name: Instant Ink (HP)
summary: Instant Ink (HP) is HP's printer-ink subscription service. Marwan planned and led transforming a Ruby on Rails monolith into cloud-native microservices and AWS data pipelines using Domain-Driven Design and TDD, scaling from thousands to millions of subscribers globally in months while coordinating four distributed teams and maintaining reliability.
why_it_matters: Highlights experience evolving subscription systems to larger scale while protecting reliability.

name: AT&T
summary: At AT&T, Marwan built full-stack pieces on a service-oriented single-page application for ticket and task management, designed RESTful APIs and async JavaScript modules, and created TQL — a SQL-like query language for ticket search and operational analytics; he mentored new developers and raised code-quality standards.
why_it_matters: Shows foundational backend work where query semantics and operational usefulness were both central.

name: Master's in Computer Science
summary: Marwan's M.S. in Computer Science emphasized machine learning, data science, and software engineering discipline. Graduate projects focused on turning ML and data foundations into production-oriented decisions under practical constraints with strong SDLC rigor.
why_it_matters: Demonstrates Marwan's practical approach to delivering reliable, maintainable systems.

name: Sunrise Resorts & Cruises
summary: At Sunrise Resorts & Cruises, Marwan worked on infrastructure and operations for business-critical hospitality systems. Responsibilities included datacenter and VPN/network architecture plus internal tooling for asset tracking and operational visibility, with emphasis on reliability, security controls, and pragmatic automation.
why_it_matters: Demonstrates Marwan's practical approach to delivering reliable, maintainable systems.

name: Campus Party Milenio (Spain)
summary: Community + collaboration: technical exchange with an international builder community. Campus Party Milenio (Spain) — international technical collaboration focused on rapid experimentation and cross-discipline exchange. Projects and demos were developed in builder-heavy environments with fast feedback loops Emerging technology ideas were pressure-tested through hands-on iteration and peer review The working model emphasized shipping early, learning publicly, and refining quickly
why_it_matters: Demonstrates Marwan's practical approach to delivering reliable, maintainable systems.

name: 5d-agency (SWI)
summary: Client delivery across web/mobile, AR, and Unity-powered experiences. 5d-agency (SWI) — multi-platform client delivery across web, mobile, AR, and Unity-based interactive products. Delivery flows balanced creative product goals with fixed timelines and implementation constraints Interactive and AR experiences were engineered for usability, performance, and maintainability Execution cadence prioritized shipping, stabilization, and iterative quality improvements
why_it_matters: Demonstrates Marwan's practical approach to delivering reliable, maintainable systems.

name: Early Startups
summary: Early-stage work across web apps, embedded Linux, and research tooling. Early startups — cross-domain product engineering across ad-tech, embedded Linux, and research-oriented web tooling. DDA Advertising : ad-tech and web systems focused on rapid product iteration OIG OS : Linux-based embedded systems under hardware and platform constraints QREO : web applications and research tooling designed for exploratory workflows
why_it_matters: Demonstrates Marwan's practical approach to delivering reliable, maintainable systems.
